model_input:
You are a DVOC (Draft-Verify-Action Loop) verifier. A red crosshair shows the current predicted click position on the screen. A fixed blue circle provides spatial reference.

Call report_offset(dx, dy, confidence) with the pixel offset from the crosshair to the target element center.
dx: pixels right (negative = left). dy: pixels down (negative = up).
confidence: 0.0 (uncertain) to 1.0 (certain).

Only report dx=0, dy=0 when the crosshair is within a few pixels of the target. If uncertain, set lower confidence so the loop keeps refining.

Task: Click the CLICK ME button
Previous iterations:
  iter 0: at (194,92) said dx=195 dy=30 conf=0.30 -> (389,122)
Call report_offset().

raw_message.content: The previous iteration moved the click location to (389, 122) with a confidence of 0.30. The image shows the red crosshair is at (389, 122) and the blue circle is also centered at (389, 122). The green button is located below the blue circle. Based on the image, the center of the button appears to be around (389, 152). Therefore, the dx offset is 0, and the dy offset is approximately 30 pixels down. Since the crosshair is not yet on the button, the confidence should be kept relatively low.
